texts.label: text
texts.orig: In December 2003, a Healthcare Inspection by the Department of Veteran Affairs Office of Inspector General was conducted on the Homemaker/Home Health Aide Program to evaluate Department of Veteran Affairs (VA) Medical facilities compliance with the Veteran Health Administration (VHA) policy for providing services to patients that were clinically appropriate, cost effective, and met customer expectations. The outcome of that Inspection revealed a lack of supportable data for placement of patients.
texts.text: In December 2003, a Healthcare Inspection by the Department of Veteran Affairs Office of Inspector General was conducted on the Homemaker/Home Health Aide Program to evaluate Department of Veteran Affairs (VA) Medical facilities compliance with the Veteran Health Administration (VHA) policy for providing services to patients that were clinically appropriate, cost effective, and met customer expectations. The outcome of that Inspection revealed a lack of supportable data for placement of patients.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As part of the Undersecretary for Health’s commitment to complying with VA policy, the need for accurately documenting and reporting patient placements is needed. VA Geriatric Extended Care (VA GEC) Reports will serve as an interim reporting method until the national rollup of VA GEC Referral data is provided through Computerized Patient Record System Reengineered (CPRS-R). VA GEC Reports will display the percent of patients referred to select GEC programs who meet the eligibility criteria as outlined in the Under Secretary for Health’s Information Letter IL 10-2003-005 and VHA Handbook 1140.2.
texts.text: As part of the Undersecretary for Health’s commitment to complying with VA policy, the need for accurately documenting and reporting patient placements is needed. VA Geriatric Extended Care (VA GEC) Reports will serve as an interim reporting method until the national rollup of VA GEC Referral data is provided through Computerized Patient Record System Reengineered (CPRS-R). VA GEC Reports will display the percent of patients referred to select GEC programs who meet the eligibility criteria as outlined in the Under Secretary for Health’s Information Letter IL 10-2003-005 and VHA Handbook 1140.2.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VA GEC Reports will provide quarterly statistical reports on the following VA funded programs.
texts.text: VA GEC Reports will provide quarterly statistical reports on the following VA funded programs.

texts.orig: The stakeholder will create an Excel spreadsheet for the purpose of importing the data received from the local sites, according to the specifications provided in the Algorithm that follows.
texts.text: The stakeholder will create an Excel spreadsheet for the purpose of importing the data received from the local sites, according to the specifications provided in the Algorithm that follows.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Local sites must task the job by setting the queue to automatically generate the quarterly reports.
texts.text: Local sites must task the job by setting the queue to automatically generate the quarterly reports.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Office of Geriatric and Extended Care is responsible for importing data received, via electronic email, into a GEC created excel spreadsheet.
texts.text: The Office of Geriatric and Extended Care is responsible for importing data received, via electronic email, into a GEC created excel spreadsheet.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The main feature of this product is the creation of a report that will allow monitoring compliance of VA patients referred to specified VA funded Geriatric Extended Care programs.
texts.text: The main feature of this product is the creation of a report that will allow monitoring compliance of VA patients referred to specified VA funded Geriatric Extended Care programs.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Create a tool that will extract the data on patients meeting the criteria from the VA funded GEC program.
texts.text: Create a tool that will extract the data on patients meeting the criteria from the VA funded GEC program.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The extracted data will be emailed to appropriate compliance office.
texts.text: The extracted data will be emailed to appropriate compliance office.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: GEC Fiscal Quarterly Rollup [PXRM GEC2 QUARTERLY ROLLUP]
texts.text: GEC Fiscal Quarterly Rollup [PXRM GEC2 QUARTERLY ROLLUP]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This is a queueable option that will gather and report to Washington DC the Fiscal Quarterly information. This option should never be placed on an individual’s menu. It should be scheduled for the 8th day of the first month of the next calendar quarter at any time of the facility’s choosing. The rescheduling frequency should be set to “3M” (every three months).
texts.text: This is a queueable option that will gather and report to Washington DC the Fiscal Quarterly information. This option should never be placed on an individual’s menu. It should be scheduled for the 8th day of the first month of the next calendar quarter at any time of the facility’s choosing. The rescheduling frequency should be set to “3M” (every three months).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When this mail group is installed, it will contain the email address of the two individuals in Washington DC who will receive the quarterly data. These names should not be removed. Names of local individuals (for example, CACs) may be added, if they desire to receive these reports.
texts.text: When this mail group is installed, it will contain the email address of the two individuals in Washington DC who will receive the quarterly data. These names should not be removed. Names of local individuals (for example, CACs) may be added, if they desire to receive these reports.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We recommend thorough testing of GEC reminder dialogs by staff prior to implementation to avoid GEC report roll-up inaccuracies. Testing of GEC reminder dialogs and reports in a test account should mimic the actual processes and workload capture used in the site's production environment.
texts.text: We recommend thorough testing of GEC reminder dialogs by staff prior to implementation to avoid GEC report roll-up inaccuracies. Testing of GEC reminder dialogs and reports in a test account should mimic the actual processes and workload capture used in the site's production environment.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Informatics staff and GEC referral staff should work together to identify potential issues that arise during testing that may require modification of clinical processes and/or workload capture. Accurate capture and reporting of GEC referral health factors may require careful analysis of workload capture processes at sites that use Event Capture software. Inaccurate reporting may lead to questions from the Inspector General’s office concerning funding for the patients referred to the “Home Help” type of programs.
texts.text: Informatics staff and GEC referral staff should work together to identify potential issues that arise during testing that may require modification of clinical processes and/or workload capture. Accurate capture and reporting of GEC referral health factors may require careful analysis of workload capture processes at sites that use Event Capture software. Inaccurate reporting may lead to questions from the Inspector General’s office concerning funding for the patients referred to the “Home Help” type of programs.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Potential issues if you use Event Capture
texts.text: Potential issues if you use Event Capture

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Event capture does not pass workload to PCE in real time. Data is not passed to PCE until after hours, so this needs to be taken into account when testing.
texts.text: Event capture does not pass workload to PCE in real time. Data is not passed to PCE until after hours, so this needs to be taken into account when testing.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: There are several steps where real front-line users could make minor mistakes that would result in data entry/workload not matching up with the Care coordination note.
texts.text: There are several steps where real front-line users could make minor mistakes that would result in data entry/workload not matching up with the Care coordination note.
